text.title: Verbatim → Backlog — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules covering the 4-step transform, verbatim attachment, and the Job-Story framing.

rule.statement: The transform is EXACTLY 4 steps in order: (1) EXTRACT pain statements with timestamps and source IDs, (2) CLUSTER by embedding similarity (threshold 0.78 default), (3) FRAME each cluster as a Job-Story, (4) PRIORITISE with RICE. Skipping CLUSTER produces 50+ duplicate cards; skipping FRAME loses the user-context.

rationale.source: Teresa Torres — Continuous Discovery Habits / Rob Fitzpatrick — The Mom Test

rule.rationale: Each step removes a class of failure: extract removes implicit pain inference, cluster removes duplication noise, frame restores user-context, prioritise creates a working order. Skipping any of the four collapses the pipeline.

rule.statement: Every backlog card MUST have ≥ 2 verbatim quotes attached, each with `source_id`, `source_type ∈ {interview, support, discord, dm, review}`, and `timestamp`. Cards with < 2 quotes are quarantined as `evidence_insufficient`.

rationale.source: Bob Moesta JTBD / Mom-Test compliance research

rule.rationale: Two quotes establish that the pain is non-idiosyncratic. Single-quote cards drive feature-thrash (build for one loud user). Attached source IDs allow re-listening when planning.

rule.statement: Clustering uses cosine similarity threshold 0.78 by default; tuning allowed ∈ [0.72, 0.85] per operator. Clusters smaller than 2 statements are HELD as "watch-list" (not cards); clusters > 12 statements are SPLIT (likely 2 jobs merged).
rule.rationale: 0.78 is the empirically robust threshold for English customer feedback (above 0.85 over-splits, below 0.72 over-merges). Tuning band reflects domain variation.

rule.statement: Card title MUST use Job-Story format: "When <trigger>, I want <motivation>, so I can <outcome>." Forbidden alternatives: "As a <persona>..." (loses trigger), "Improve onboarding" (no who/when), "Fix bug XYZ" (not a story).

rationale.source: Alan Klement — Replacing the User Story / Intercom Jobs Stories framework

rule.rationale: Persona-based user stories embed assumed buyer roles; Job-Stories center the trigger and circumstance, matching the verbatim pain in the source quote.

rule.statement: Cards score on RICE: REACH (% of users affected, from cluster size / active users), IMPACT (per-user severity 1-3), CONFIDENCE (% based on quote count + recency), EFFORT (T-shirt sizes XS=0.5 / S=1 / M=3 / L=8 / XL=21 person-days). RICE = (R × I × C) / E. All four inputs MUST be sourced.
rule.rationale: RICE is the simplest model that survives founder pressure to over-weight any single dimension. Untyped inputs lead to manipulation; sourced inputs preserve the rigor.

example.title: From inbox → cluster → card
example.code:
EXTRACT (3 statements from a single week): S1 [support#214, 2026-05-12]: "I had to export the dashboard to PDF for my CFO; took 20 minutes manually." S2 [interview#7, 2026-05-13]: "Every Friday I export the dashboard and share it. PDF option is buried." S3 [discord#1402, 2026-05-15]: "Can we get a one-click PDF export? My weekly report takes longer than the analysis itself."
CLUSTER (cosine sim 0.82, above 0.78 threshold): Cluster: "weekly-pdf-export" (3 statements)
FRAME (Job-Story per r4): Title: "When I prepare my weekly report, I want a one-click PDF export, so I can spend time on analysis instead of formatting."
PRIORITISE: R = 3/120 active users this week = 2.5% (3 distinct mentions in trailing 7d; 120 actives) I = 2 (productivity loss, not blocker) C = 80% (3 quotes, all within 7d → high recency) E = M (3 person-days for PDF render + email) RICE = (2.5 × 2 × 0.80) / 3 = 1.33
Card: backlog/CARD-447 with R, I, C, E sourced. Quotes S1-S3 attached with source URLs.
example.note: r1-r5 all visible: ordered transform, verbatim, cluster, frame, scored.

example.title: PM-language card without verbatim (r2 + r4 violation)
example.code:
Card title: "Improve export functionality"
No quotes attached. No source IDs. Persona = "user". RICE: R=10% (made up), I=3, C=50%, E=L → RICE = 1.875.
6 weeks later: nobody remembers why this was prioritised. The actual pain (Friday-CFO-PDF) is lost; team builds CSV + XLS + JSON exports instead.
example.note: Generic backlog rot. The verbatim attachment is what saves the original signal.
